business.headline: RBI updates e-mandate rules for transactions over Rs 15,000. Check detailsUnder the new guidelines, recurring payments of up to Rs 15,000 will not require a one-time password (OTP). However, transactions above this threshold will need additional authentication.

Read on to know why demand destruction may be needed to restore balance between oil supply and demand.

Apple shares rise 1% after Tim Cook exit, John Ternus named CEOThe stock closed at $273.05, up $2.82 or 1.04%, reflecting a measured but positive reaction from investors to the unexpected leadership change as John Ternus steps up to lead Apple.

Who is John Ternus, the man set to replace Tim Cook at AppleTernus will take over from September 1, while Cook will move into the role of executive chairman, marking a shift in leadership at one of the world's most valuable companies.

general news.headline: India will not bow to any form of terror: PM Modi on Pahalgam attack anniversaryPM Modi, in a post on X, paid tribute to the victims of the Pahalgam terror attack, in which terrorists killed 26 people, mostly tourists, saying they "will never be forgotten."

Live: US blocks dollar shipments to Iraq to pressure Iran-backed militiasThe fragile ceasefire in the US-Israel war on Iran has been extended, offering a narrow window for diplomacy even as uncertainty persists over whether talks can prevent a return to conflict. US President Donald Trump has indicated he remains prepared to resume military action if negotiations fail, while simultaneously calling for a unified proposal from Tehran. Efforts to restart dialogue, however, remain uneven. Iran has yet to confirm participation in proposed talks in Pakistan, and US Vice President JD Vance's planned visit to Islamabad has now been indefinitely cancelled. Tensions continue to simmer around the Strait of Hormuz, where the US naval blockade remains a key flashpoint, viewed by Tehran as a breach of ceasefire terms. Beyond the diplomatic impasse, the wider region remains volatile. Israeli operations have continued in Lebanon despite a ceasefire, with Beirut calling for internal unity ahead of fresh engagements with Israel in Washington. As the extended deadline unfolds, the risk of escalation remains high if diplomatic momentum fails to translate into a concrete agreement. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

Perfume, ink, glue on EVMs will be tampering: EC before Bengal, Tamil Nadu pollsThe poll panel warned that applying fragrances on EVM buttons to track votes violates ballot secrecy, and directed officials to inspect machines thoroughly, ensuring all buttons remain visible and free of any substances.
